summary: Iteration 4 — Frontend ONLY. Verified the two Markdown/Resource viewer bug fixes (remark-gfm + MarkdownToPDF rewrite for tables/code). Authenticated as admin (benitezl@go.ugr.es) by minting a JWT via MAGIC_LINK_SECRET → POST /api/auth/verify and injecting into localStorage.lcd_token. Tested 4 resources end-to-end with Playwright (1440x900): (1) /recurso/plantillas-system-prompt-a1-c2 — page renders, 18 fenced code blocks rendered as <pre>, NO horizontal page overflow (docScrollW=1440==winW), <pre> blocks have computed overflow-x:auto and word-break:break-word so long prompts scroll INSIDE the box, Navbar still anchored at top:43px width:1440, PDF button transitions to a blob: href with text '📄 Descargar PDF' (never 'Error al generar PDF'). (2) /recurso/rubrica-evaluacion-modulo-i — 7 GFM tables rendered with <thead> + 31 <tbody><tr>, zero raw pipes leaked into innerText, PDF blob generated successfully. (3) /recurso/lectura-1-etica-ia-educacion-ele — non-downloadable: body renders, PDF button correctly absent. (4) /recurso/rubrica-evaluacion-modulo-ii spot-check — PDF blob generated. (5) /dashboard regression — loads, shows 'Mis cursos' with IA para la enseñanza de ELE, progress bar 0%, founder-paid badge, no errors. ZERO pageerror events and ZERO console errors across the entire run. Both bug fixes are confirmed working.
test_report_links: /app/backend/tests/conftest.py (reused make_magic_token helper to mint admin JWT)
critical_code_review_comments: Resource.jsx MarkdownToPDF table parser line 153: detection requires line.trim().startsWith('|'). GFM permits tables that start without a leading '|' (e.g. 'Col1 | Col2'). Such tables would NOT be detected by this custom PDF parser and would fall through to plain <Text> paragraphs in the PDF — though they still render correctly in the on-screen ReactMarkdown via remark-gfm. Low-priority because the seeded materials all use leading '|' style.

Resource.jsx MarkdownToPDF inline code regex (line 72) uses non-greedy [^*]+ / [^_]+ / [^`]+ — this means a single line containing '**bold** plain **bold2**' will be parsed correctly, but a single asterisk inside text like 'a*b' (no closing) is left intact (ok). However bullet detection at line 166 uses /^[-*]\s+(.*)/ which can mis-classify a line like '*important*' as a bullet when written at start of line without the trailing space — currently safe because seeded MD doesn't do this.

Resource.jsx line 246 .lesson-body — the on-screen container has overflow controlled per-element (pre/code) via index.css; verified in DOM that <pre> has overflow-x:auto AND word-break:break-word. No issue, but consider also adding `max-width: 100%` to <table> elements rendered by remark-gfm so an extremely wide table (10+ columns) also scrolls horizontally instead of stretching the container. Not currently triggered by seeded content (max ~5 cols).

PDFDownloadLink (line 232-243) re-renders the <Document> on EVERY parent re-render which is expensive. The doc is currently only computed when `data` changes (downloadable depends on data via useMemo) so it's fine, but consider memoizing the <Document> JSX with useMemo(() => <ResourcePDF ... />, [data]) to avoid re-rendering during unrelated state changes.

Token key in /app/frontend/src/lib/auth.jsx is `lcd_token`, but the review_request mentions `clm_token` — minor doc drift; fixed by reading auth.jsx directly. Worth aligning the review request template with the actual codebase string.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5 scenarios pass: plantilla, rubrica-I tables, lectura non-downloadable, rubrica-II PDF, dashboard regression)

test_credentials: Admin: benitezl@go.ugr.es. JWT minted in-process via MAGIC_LINK_SECRET → POST /api/auth/verify (same flow as conftest.make_magic_token), then injected as localStorage.lcd_token before navigating to /recurso/...
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded resources only (no DB writes from frontend tests).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend auth pattern that worked: (1) page.goto root, (2) page.evaluate(`() => localStorage.setItem('lcd_token', '<jwt>')`), (3) navigate to /recurso/<slug>. The JWT is generated programmatically with `make_magic_token(email)` from /app/backend/tests/conftest.py then exchanged at POST /api/auth/verify for a session JWT. To verify PDFDownloadLink success: poll the <a data-testid='resource-download-pdf'> for href.startsWith('blob:') with a 12s budget — once the blob is ready, innerText is '📄 Descargar PDF'; on failure it would be 'Error al generar PDF'. To verify GFM tables: count document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid="resource-body"] table') > 0 AND count innerText raw pipes == 0. To verify no overflow: docScrollW === window.innerWidth. Resources known to contain GFM tables: rubrica-evaluacion-modulo-i (7 tables, 31 rows). Resources with long fenced code: plantillas-system-prompt-a1-c2 (18 code blocks).
rca of the issue: No issues found in iter4. Both reported bugs from the previous iteration are confirmed FIXED: (a) overflow — index.css `.lesson-body pre/code` now uses overflow-x:auto + word-break:break-word so long prompts scroll inside their <pre> rather than pushing the page; (b) PDF crash on tables/code — the rewritten MarkdownToPDF in Resource.jsx (lines 86-177) successfully buffers and flushes both ```fenced``` and `|table|` blocks, producing a valid blob: URL within ~1s on both rubrica (7 tables) and plantilla (18 code blocks) resources.
